title: BMS-564929
description: BMS-564929 is a potent, orally active nonsteroidal selective androgen receptor modulator (SARM) designed to treat age-related declines in muscle mass and physical function. It works by acting as a high-affinity agonist for androgen receptors in muscle and bone while maintaining significant tissue selectivity to avoid stimulating the prostate.

research.title: Ockham's Razor and Selective Androgen Receptor Modulators (SARMs): Are We Overlooking the Role of 5α-Reductase?
research.summary: This study explains how SARMs like BMS-564929 achieve selectivity because they are not substrates for 5α-reductase, preventing the localized amplification of androgenic effects in the prostate.

research.title: Selective Androgen Receptor Modulators (SARMs) as Function Promoting Therapies
research.summary: Research indicates that nonsteroidal SARMs act as full agonists in muscle and bone while acting as only partial agonists in the prostate, offering a safer profile than traditional steroids.

research.title: Selective androgen receptor modulators: the future of androgen therapy?
research.summary: Clinical analysis suggests that SARMs represent a promising class of drugs for treating muscle wasting and hypogonadism with significantly reduced side effects compared to testosterone.

sentiment.summary: While clinical research is highly optimistic about its tissue selectivity, user data for this specific compound is sparse compared to more common SARMs, leading to a cautious but generally curious sentiment regarding its muscle-building potential.

effects.label: High Potency:
effects.description: In preclinical models, it has shown to be more potent than testosterone in stimulating muscle growth per milligram

effects.label: Tissue Selectivity:
effects.description: Highly effective at separating muscle-building benefits from undesirable androgenic side effects

effects.label: Oral Bioavailability:
effects.description: Unlike many traditional androgens, it is designed for effective oral administration

dosage.label: Clinical Trials:
dosage.description: Doses in human research have been evaluated at very low levels (e.g., 0.1 mg to 1 mg) due to its high potency

dosage.label: Preclinical Range:
dosage.description: Animal studies utilized doses ranging from 0.03 to 3 mg/kg to determine anabolic thresholds

dosage.label: Experimental Use:
dosage.description: Users often caution that due to high binding affinity, 'less is more' to maintain selectivity

sideEffects.label: Hormonal Suppression:
sideEffects.description: Like all SARMs, it may suppress natural testosterone production at higher dosages

sideEffects.label: Lipid Changes:
sideEffects.description: Potential reduction in HDL (good) cholesterol, a common trait across the SARM class

sideEffects.label: SHBG Reduction:
sideEffects.description: Likely to cause a decrease in Sex Hormone-Binding Globulin levels during active use

availability.label: Legal Status:
availability.description: Currently not FDA-approved for human use and prohibited by WADA for competitive athletes

availability.label: Research Only:
availability.description: Primarily available through chemical supply companies for laboratory research purposes
